audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim Gideon call, altar destruction, and fleece-sign narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Midianite oppression, prophet rebuke, angel of YHWH, Gideon's fear and protest, altar meal sign, Baal altar destruction by night, Joash's defense, Jerubbaal naming, Spirit-clothing, tribal summons, and fleece signs.

verses.restored: Children of Israel did evil in eyes of YHWH, and YHWH gave them into hand of Midian seven years.
Hand of Midian was strong over Israel; because of Midian, children of Israel made for themselves dens in mountains, caves, and strongholds.
It was, when Israel sowed, that Midian, Amalek, and sons of east came up against them.
They camped against them and destroyed produce of land as far as Gaza; they left no sustenance in Israel, neither sheep nor ox nor donkey.
For they and their livestock came up, and their tents; they came like locusts for multitude, and they and their camels were without number; they came into land to ruin it.
Israel became very low because of Midian, and children of Israel cried out to YHWH.
It was, when children of Israel cried out to YHWH because of Midian,
that YHWH sent a man, a prophet, to children of Israel. He said to them, Thus says YHWH Divine of Israel: I brought you up from Egypt and brought you out from house of slaves.
I rescued you from hand of Egypt and from hand of all oppressing you; I drove them out before you and gave you their land.
I said to you, I am YHWH your Divine; you shall not fear gods of Amorite in whose land you dwell. But you did not listen to my voice.
Messenger of YHWH came and sat under terebinth at Ophrah that belonged to Joash the Abiezrite, while Gideon his son was beating wheat in winepress to hide it from Midian.
Messenger of YHWH appeared to him and said to him, YHWH is with you, mighty man of strength.

Gideon said to him, Please, my lord, if YHWH is with us, why then has all this found us? Where are all his wonders that our fathers recounted to us, saying, Did not YHWH bring us up from Egypt? But now YHWH has abandoned us and given us into palm of Midian.
YHWH turned to him and said, Go in this strength of yours and save Israel from palm of Midian. Have I not sent you?
He said to him, Please, my lord, by what shall I save Israel? Look, my clan is weakest in Manasseh, and I am least in house of my father.
YHWH said to him, Surely I will be with you, and you shall strike Midian as one man.
He said to him, Please, if I have found favor in your eyes, make for me a sign that it is you speaking with me.
Please do not depart from here until I come to you, bring out my offering, and set it before you. He said, I will sit until you return.
Gideon went and prepared a kid of goats and unleavened cakes from an ephah of flour; meat he put in basket and broth he put in pot, brought them out to him under terebinth, and presented them.
Messenger of Divine said to him, Take meat and unleavened cakes and place them on this rock, and pour out broth. He did so.
Messenger of YHWH stretched out end of staff in his hand and touched meat and unleavened cakes; fire rose from rock and consumed meat and unleavened cakes, and messenger of YHWH went from his eyes.
Gideon saw that he was messenger of YHWH, and Gideon said, Alas, Lord YHWH! For I have seen messenger of YHWH face to face.
YHWH said to him, Peace to you; do not fear; you shall not die.
Gideon built there an altar to YHWH and called it YHWH-shalom/YHWH is peace; to this day it is still in Ophrah of Abiezrites.
It was that night YHWH said to him, Take bull of your father, the second bull seven years old, tear down altar of Baal belonging to your father, and cut down Asherah that is beside it.
Build altar to YHWH your Divine on top of this stronghold in ordered arrangement, take the second bull, and offer burnt offering with wood of Asherah that you cut down.
Gideon took ten men from his servants and did as YHWH spoke to him; but because he feared house of his father and men of city, he did it by night and not by day.
Men of city rose early in morning, and look, altar of Baal was broken down, Asherah beside it cut down, and second bull offered on altar built.
They said each to his neighbor, Who did this thing? They searched and investigated and said, Gideon son of Joash did this thing.
Men of city said to Joash, Bring out your son, and he shall die, because he broke down altar of Baal and cut down Asherah beside it.

Joash said to all who stood against him, Will you contend for Baal? Will you save him? Whoever contends for him shall be put to death by morning. If he is a god, let him contend for himself because someone broke down his altar.
On that day he called him Jerubbaal, saying, Let Baal contend against him, because he broke down his altar.
All Midian, Amalek, and sons of east gathered together, crossed over, and camped in valley of Jezreel.
Spirit of YHWH clothed Gideon; he blew shofar, and Abiezer was called after him.
He sent messengers through all Manasseh, and it too was called after him; he sent messengers through Asher, Zebulun, and Naphtali, and they came up to meet them.
Gideon said to Divine, If you are saving Israel by my hand, as you have spoken,
look, I am placing fleece of wool on threshing floor. If dew is on fleece alone and on all ground dryness, then I shall know that you will save Israel by my hand, as you have spoken.
It was so. He rose early next day, squeezed fleece, and wrung dew from fleece, a bowl full of water.

Gideon said to Divine, Let not your anger burn against me, and I will speak only this once; please let me test only this once with fleece. Please let it be dry on fleece alone, and on all ground let there be dew.
Divine did so that night: it was dry on fleece alone, and on all ground there was dew.

translation_notes: Midianite oppression is described as economic devastation: crops, animals, security, and ordinary dwelling collapse until Israel hides in caves and cries out.
Gideon's first speech resists easy triumphalism: he asks why suffering has found the people if YHWH is with them.
YHWH names Gideon 'mighty man of strength' while Gideon is hiding wheat, making courage compatible with fear and survival behavior.
Gideon's altar is called YHWH-shalom after he survives the Divine encounter; peace appears before the war narrative resumes.

The Baal/Asherah destruction is internal covenant confrontation in ancient Israelite narrative. It must not authorize modern religious vandalism, persecution, or violence against polytheists, pagans, or other faith communities.
Joash's defense—if Baal is a god, let him contend for himself—de-escalates a mob death demand, though it still contains retaliatory threat language.
The Spirit 'clothed' Gideon, a vivid embodiment image rather than possession language to exploit for coercive leadership claims.
The fleece signs show Gideon's fear and need for confirmation; they should not become a manipulative testing formula or proof that every anxious person must demand signs.
safety_note: Do not use Judges 6 to justify religious vandalism, persecution, mob punishment, coercive sign-seeking, or shaming fearful people. Center economic oppression, honest protest, courage inside fear, de-escalation of mob violence, and Divine peace before militarized action.
